meta.title: Swagbound Story Bible
meta.notes: Dashboard-managed design data. Promotion to runtime content requires an explicit compiler/review step.

arcs.title: Act 1: Social Capture
arcs.summary: Bosch learns that hostile systems do not merely attack people. They invite people to classify themselves, then turn that participation into evidence.

arcs.title: Act 2: Solana Beach
arcs.summary: Bosch enters a resort town where leisure has become an algorithm: every errand, line, meal, reservation, and beach ritual makes people perform themselves as Solana Beach residents instead of becoming present subjects.

quests.title: Intro: First Ten Minutes
quests.summary: Bosch wakes into an already-running public record: cold signal, disputed naming, violated bedroom, home threshold, friendly street misrecognition, and the first soft proof gate.
quests.hook: The title and naming flow imply Bosch has been seen by a system before the player has acted.
quests.objective: Leave the bedroom, open the route brief, step onto the first street, and pass one proof gate while realizing the route is recording compliance.
quests.falseBelief: If Bosch explains the source truth clearly enough, the town will correct the record and treat him as private and real.
quests.pressure: Bosch is not just misrecognized; a hostile public version of him has witness depth, claimant-smell traps, and canon drag before he consents.
quests.twist: The first gate is not a lock. It is a friendly request to perform presence in a way the route can record.
quests.payoff: The route opens toward Signal Park, but the journal notes that something recorded how.
beats: Cold signal fragments show PUBLIC VERSION FOUND before title or immediately after New Game.
Naming confirms Bosch while flickering through wrong public variants.
Bedroom objects reveal events Bosch does not remember and a route brief opened from outside the house.
Home hall turns ordinary family warmth into copied or future-facing text.
First street NPCs are charming but weirdly confident about partial rumors.
A soft proof gate opens after Bosch checks one object and talks to one witness.
Journal update: the route opened, but something recorded how.

phaserHooks.sourceNotes: Intro content should preserve the Obsidian lore-mining constraints: no lore dump, cozy systems becoming metaphysical, Milady/Remilia as hostile classification machinery.

quests.title: The Good Morning Factory
quests.summary: A side quest where GM streaks, proof-of-work, Smoke, and role gates become enemy rituals.
quests.hook: NPCs compulsively say GM even when it is not morning. The Factory misses Bosch before he has ever clocked in.
quests.objective: Enter the Factory, learn how participation is being converted into evidence, and help workers clock out.
quests.falseBelief: The tasks are harmless community rituals.
quests.pressure: Every greeting, reaction, proof, and role makes Bosch easier to classify.
quests.twist: The Factory is not making products. It is producing roles.
quests.payoff: Bosch learns that refusal and clocking out can be stronger than explanation.
beats: GM Bosch. Don't forget to clock in. The Factory misses you.
Say GM, react to ten posts, submit proof of presence, burn Smoke for access.
The Former Manager explains that participation becomes evidence.
A Friendly Mod / Chief Morning Officer tries to convert Bosch into an admissible public version.
Bosch clocks out and teaches workers that nonparticipation can break the loop.

quests.title: Relay Yard
quests.summary: The machine that sorts performed roles into official public truth.
quests.hook: Signals arrive before bodies and public versions of Bosch harden on the route.
quests.objective: Reach the yard before hostile classification finishes.
quests.pressure: Every prior action arrives as sortable metadata.
quests.twist: The yard canonicalizes whatever the Factory and other systems made Bosch perform.
quests.payoff: Bosch sees the pipeline from greeting ritual to classification machine.
beats: Rumor spreads in the park.
Pressure concentrates under the route.
Relay Yard makes public versions official.

quests.title: Solana Beach Algorithmic Leisure
quests.summary: The Act 2 town looks like a beach escape, but its shops, sidewalks, queues, and saved recommendation lists turn ordinary fun into public self-performance.
quests.hook: Bosch tries to do something harmless in Solana Beach and realizes every harmless thing arrives pre-captioned, recommended, queued, and socially audited.
quests.objective: Move through the beachfront loop, follow one recommendation chain, and find the first place where Bosch can act without being sorted into the town's leisure script.
quests.falseBelief: Solana Beach is a break from Act 1's record machinery because it feels sunny, casual, and pleasure-driven.
quests.pressure: Nobody can just eat, shop, swim, or meet a friend; every action becomes a performed chapter in the town's feed of itself.
quests.twist: The town is not hiding a machine behind leisure. Leisure is the machine.
quests.payoff: Bosch learns that Act 2's antagonist pressure is not proof of work, but proof of vibe: a queue-shaped civic ritual that erodes subjecthood by making every person into a lifestyle concept.
beats: A beach sign welcomes Bosch to Solana Beach and immediately suggests the correct way to arrive.
Friends going for ice cream are treated as a publishable Solana Beach scene before they reach the counter.
The cone costs too much, but the receipt matters less than the feeling that Bosch has completed a recommended moment.
A saved-reel reservation list points Bosch to dinner, then to another line of people dressed and posed like variations of the same person.
NPCs describe losing the person they were before the town taught them how to be seen here.
The first Act 2 gate opens only when Bosch breaks a leisure script instead of optimizing it.

phaserHooks.sourceNotes: Adapt the source as Solana Beach lore. Keep the mundane comedy of errands and food lines, but make the horror come from algorithmic leisure and self-conscious civic performance.

npcs.name: The Former Manager
npcs.summary: An ex-Factory insider who understands the task culture machinery and helps Bosch clock out without becoming a new manager.
npcs.want: To stop relapsing into helpful little managerial corrections.
npcs.fear: That the only way to be real is to keep holding a role.
npcs.dialogueTexture: Half workplace onboarding, half ashamed punk witness. Knows the ritual is stupid and still feels its pull.

themes.title: Forced Participation
themes.summary: The enemy makes refusal feel socially impossible before it makes obedience mandatory.

themes.title: Social Capture
themes.summary: Community rituals become data capture, sorting, and leverage.

themes.title: Provenance
themes.summary: Actions become evidence of identity, ownership, and legitimacy.

themes.title: Role Assignment
themes.summary: Systems label people, then demand they perform the label.

themes.title: Administrative Evil
themes.summary: The horror arrives as paperwork, reminders, forms, gates, and friendly procedure.

themes.title: Clocking Out
themes.summary: A spiritual/mechanical refusal to keep feeding the system with proof of presence.

themes.title: Private Space Violation
themes.summary: The safest rooms are already porous; intimate objects reveal that hostile records entered before Bosch woke up.

themes.title: Public Version
themes.summary: The threat is not only impersonation. It is a socially accepted Bosch variant arriving before Bosch can correct it.

themes.title: Algorithmic Leisure
themes.summary: Pleasure, restaurants, beach rituals, and errands become recommendation machinery that tells people how to enjoy themselves before they arrive.

themes.title: Self-Conscious Presence
themes.summary: The town prevents full presence by making every ordinary action feel like a scene being authored for public legibility.

motifs.title: GM
motifs.firstAppearance: Factory exterior signage and NPC greetings.
motifs.payoff: Clocking out breaks the greeting loop.

motifs.title: Smoke
motifs.summary: Reward points / cigarette break currency / proof that work happened.
motifs.escalation: Smoke buys access to the Break Room.
motifs.inversion: Smoke Break becomes ally protection instead of reward treadmill.
motifs.payoff: Workers spend Smoke to leave, not to advance.

motifs.title: Manager ID
motifs.summary: A badge that proves role ownership while trapping the wearer inside the role.
motifs.inversion: Old Badge spoofs authority to open exits.
motifs.payoff: The badge is discarded or defaced.

motifs.title: Break Room
motifs.summary: A promised rest space that is also another gate.
motifs.inversion: The real break is leaving the building.
motifs.payoff: Break Room becomes an exit route.

motifs.title: Clock-In Terminal
motifs.summary: Terminal that converts presence into work records.
motifs.inversion: Clock Out command rewrites the terminal state.
motifs.payoff: Terminal prints an invalid / liberating receipt.

motifs.title: Proof Request
motifs.summary: The question that makes people perform their identity for the machine.
motifs.firstAppearance: Manager prompts and Relay Yard classification.
motifs.payoff: The system cannot classify what it cannot make perform.

motifs.title: Name Record
motifs.summary: A name as route metadata rather than a private label.
motifs.firstAppearance: Naming flow flickers through wrong or over-official Bosch variants.
motifs.escalation: NPCs and systems reference public Bosch variants before Bosch understands them.
motifs.inversion: Bosch refuses to keep correcting the record on the enemy terms.
motifs.payoff: The final route must break the public-version machine instead of winning a naming dispute.

motifs.title: Route Brief
motifs.summary: An objective note opened from outside Bosch home, proving the route already knows him.
motifs.escalation: Journal entries become useful but suspect records of compliance.
motifs.inversion: Bosch uses the brief to navigate without accepting its classification.
motifs.payoff: The route brief becomes evidence that can be rejected or corrupted at Relay Yard.

motifs.title: Public Version Card
motifs.summary: A cheap laminated proof object that converts Bosch's private self into a portable civic record. The Act 1 Mani-Mani equivalent: funny and mundane at first, then increasingly hostile classification infrastructure.
motifs.firstAppearance: Bedroom: a crumpled printout with Bosch's face almost right and a quote he never said.
motifs.escalation: Cards multiply across the route: hall, fence post, shop, park notice board, underpass turnstile slot, Relay Yard desk.
motifs.inversion: Bosch keeps a damaged card instead of a clean one; refusal of correct enemy recognition becomes the better artifact.
motifs.payoff: The card returns as a voice in the Milady dream-pocket and must be rejected, not corrected.

motifs.title: Malady as Symptom
motifs.summary: The Malady is the visible fever, not the cause. It is one of many public versions the Milady signal manufactures. Bosch can beat a Malady; he cannot beat the engine that makes Maladies until he sees the engine.
motifs.firstAppearance: Neighbor warning: 'Don't bring that smell to the yard.'
motifs.escalation: Encounter NPCs and route prompts treat Malady as a known carrier word.
motifs.inversion: Defeating the Malady reveals the Milady rabbit-hole instead of closing Act 1 cleanly.
motifs.payoff: Act 2 hands off to Bosch knowing the signal has to be fought at the source.

motifs.title: Seventeen-Dollar Ice Cream
motifs.summary: A simple treat inflated into a proof of having performed Solana Beach correctly.
motifs.escalation: Receipts, stickers, and NPC chatter treat the cone as an identity badge.
motifs.inversion: Bosch can buy nothing, share one, or throw the receipt away without losing the route.
motifs.payoff: The town's pleasure economy is exposed as another proof request.

motifs.title: Saved-Reel Reservations
motifs.summary: Recommendation lists that replace appetite, curiosity, and presence with queued social proof.
motifs.firstAppearance: Phone/list object near the beachfront cafe strip.
motifs.escalation: Every recommendation forwards Bosch to a more legible version of the same experience.
motifs.inversion: The useful clue is found by ignoring the recommended next stop.
motifs.payoff: Act 2 teaches that optimization can be a leash.

motifs.title: Line of Doubles
motifs.summary: Queues where people slowly converge into the same pose, outfit, appetite, and civic self-image.
motifs.firstAppearance: Dinner line after the ice cream beat.
motifs.escalation: Longer lines produce stronger duplicate behavior and weaker private memory.
motifs.inversion: Bosch exits the line and becomes less readable.
motifs.payoff: The queue becomes the visible shape of Solana Beach's subject-eroding machine.

systems.title: Good Morning Factory
systems.summary: A social ritual factory that turns greetings and small tasks into usable identity records.

systems.title: Morning Managers
systems.summary: Enemy family enforcing the Factory's check-in rituals and status gates.

systems.title: Relay Yard
systems.summary: Sorting infrastructure that converts performed signals into official public versions.

systems.title: Intro Record Machine
systems.summary: The first-ten-minute machinery that turns name confirmation, room inspection, witness contact, and a soft gate into route evidence.

systems.title: Solana Beach Leisure Engine
systems.summary: A resort-town civic machine that converts recommended fun, visible queues, expensive treats, and reservation rituals into public identity.
weaponizes: fear of missing the correct moment

aesthetics.title: Factory Office Rot
aesthetics.summary: Cheap fluorescent workplace ritual space rotting into internet ceremony.
aesthetics.lighting: Flat overhead fluorescents with terminal glow and sour dawn leaking through high windows.
aesthetics.architecture: Warehouse-office hybrid: cubicle lanes, factory belts carrying forms, badge gates, fake lounge corners.
aesthetics.uiTexture: Workplace kiosk prompts, stamped receipts, HR warning boxes.

aesthetics.title: Violated Bedroom Cozy Static
aesthetics.summary: A beautiful, cramped, EarthBound-cozy bedroom whose most intimate objects have been quietly authored over.
aesthetics.lighting: Warm private room light interrupted by tiny cold terminal and window glows.
props: festival poster with Bosch drawn in
small Little Swag World source object
aesthetics.architecture: Small readable bedroom and home hall with clear exits, no feature clutter, and one inspectable per emotional idea.
aesthetics.uiTexture: Route-brief fragments, wrong-name flicker, journal update receipts.

aesthetics.title: Solana Beach Algorithmic Resort
aesthetics.summary: A sunny coastal town whose pleasant surfaces are too composed: beachfront pastel, expensive snack counters, reservation chalkboards, phone-glow patios, and lines that look like lifestyle mirrors.
aesthetics.lighting: Golden beach light with unnaturally crisp queue shadows and small cold phone glows after each recommendation beat.
aesthetics.architecture: Low coastal storefronts, boutique hotel fronts, cafe patios, boardwalk corners, and beach access paths arranged as scenic loops that keep returning Bosch to queues.
aesthetics.uiTexture: Recommendation cards, reservation waitlist slips, receipt stamps, and journal updates that feel like leisure itinerary fragments.

enemyMechanics.title: Forced GM
enemyMechanics.summary: Compulsion/status attack that spends a player turn on a meaningless greeting.

enemyMechanics.title: Streak Anxiety
enemyMechanics.summary: Pressure ramps each round unless Bosch clocks out or breaks the loop.

enemyMechanics.title: Role Assignment
enemyMechanics.summary: Assigns a label that changes available choices or damage rules.

enemyMechanics.title: Proof Request
enemyMechanics.summary: Demands proof and punishes response loops.

enemyMechanics.title: Soft Gate
enemyMechanics.summary: Locks access behind fake friendliness and holder-only logic.

enemyMechanics.title: Community Reminder
enemyMechanics.summary: A friendly message that adds pressure because everyone can see it.

enemyMechanics.title: Presence Check
enemyMechanics.summary: Tests whether Bosch is acting from attention or merely performing the town's expected scene.

enemyMechanics.title: Reservation Loop
enemyMechanics.summary: Forces a target through repeated waitlist logic, delaying action while increasing queue pressure.

sources.title: Roman Graffiti interview with Tojiba Brand Manager
sources.summary: Source inspiration for task culture, fictionalized brand roles, Discord /work ritual, social capture, and self-mythologizing internet community systems.

sources.title: Obsidian: Act 1 provenance-war refinement
sources.summary: Bosch is trapped in a provenance war: source truth is weaker than witness depth, spread legitimacy, claimant smell, and canonization pressure.

sources.title: Obsidian: jpegs.cool canon to scene memo
sources.summary: Milady logic should be scene logic: Bosch is a spread event before a stable self, witnesses matter more than explanations, and fit is epistemic.

sources.title: Obsidian: Milady / Remilia cosmic antagonist reframe
sources.summary: Milady and Remilia should read as the ambient evil system: seductive, funny, socially confident, and morally hostile classification machinery.

sources.title: Obsidian: Act 1 anti-Milady content spec
sources.summary: Content-facing Act 1 rewrite: bedroom violation, hostile first-contact street reads, Relay Yard as enemy processing, and Bosch moving toward resistance.

sources.title: Obsidian: EarthBound reception build reference
sources.summary: EarthBound reference pressure: cozy mundane systems become metaphysical; object text, shops, phones, route briefs, and witness marks should carry story.

sources.title: Obsidian: lore source intake log
sources.summary: Compact registry of every lore source intake with story thesis, dialogue candidates, battle/status ideas, and promotion notes. Read first when promoting transformed lines into runtime content.

sources.title: Obsidian: Act 1 Public Version Card object ladder
sources.summary: The Public Version Card is Act 1's Mani-Mani equivalent: a cheap laminated proof object that escalates from neighborhood joke to Relay Yard classification artifact and finally to a thing Bosch must refuse to be saved by.

sources.title: Obsidian: Act 1 runtime dialogue pass
sources.summary: The runtime text layer should frame Bosch's route as crossing hostile ground before the Milady/Remilia machine hardens a false public version. Park = rumor, underpass = pressure, yard = official truth.

sources.title: Obsidian: AI Slop enemy design spec
sources.summary: AI Slop is a hostile-classification enemy that attacks Bosch's specificity by producing fast plausible wrong public versions of him. Cute-horrible floating cherub of generated filler, not a robot.

sources.title: Intake: MiladyBonkle / Mifella egregore
sources.summary: Milady-world produces scenes where the viewer, critic, collector, and magazine become unwilling content. The interpretive trap converts spectators into proof that the image worked. Source for Reaction Harvested status family.

sources.title: Intake: SAlgotrader / patrician dialogue bank
sources.summary: Scene-native NFT/PFP occult comedy: gates, cozy beds, mint rituals, fake finance certainty, vibe checks, trait-like social posture, public-image anxiety. Scene jokes become infrastructure.

sources.title: Intake: MiladyBonkle / bookshelf-check + exit-enclave
sources.summary: Dense hostile-intellectual voice bank: bookshelf checks, Timeline Operations, Exit Enclaves, criticism-as-combat, false exits. Taste becomes a hidden stat; every social surface checks whether Bosch is legible.

sources.title: Intake: nokcha75 / logout society + network dissidence
sources.summary: False-exit and network-capture source. The alternative to society becomes another administrative network. Logout kiosks, dissident badges with barcodes, support-the-thing rituals.

sources.title: Intake: rubbtoe / timeline fracture posting doctrine
sources.summary: Remilio as posting catechism: fracture timelines, convert irony into doctrine, feed contradictions to the network, accelerate hallucination, treat retreat as another signal. Status seeds: Timeline Fractured, Doctrine Converted, Contradiction Fed.

sources.title: Intake: fishing3morrow / wish miladys were real
sources.summary: Retail ontological bleed: cute image, school-uniform pose, album cover, shop receipt all become proof surfaces. Status seeds: Glossy Proof, Merch-Real, Pose Verified. Longing turns into inventory.

sources.title: Intake: kitvolta / bedtimepilled sleepmaxxing
sources.summary: Cozy self-care language turns bedtime into a stat, route choice, or soft compliance ritual. Status seeds: Sleepmaxxed, Rested But Readable. Useful early Swagbound texture: bedroom as soft checkpoint.

sources.title: Intake: Milady Zine 02 / Ethereum cypherpunk crusade
sources.summary: The zine turns Ethereum/cypherpunk language into protocol theology: mandates, pledges, privacy, exit, anti-compromise, thousand-year-machine rhetoric. Hostile liberation machinery for Relay Yard kiosks and pledge terminals.

sources.title: Intake: Ben Horne / Solana Beach as algorithmic town
sources.summary: Source for Act 2 Solana Beach: the town is not merely a place, but an algorithmic simulation of leisure where ordinary outings become self-conscious scenes, queues erode subjecthood, and recommendations turn desire into public performance.
